key_insight: A sequential replica-exchange scheme with solute tempering builds supramolecular polymers one monomer at a time, enabling efficient atomistic structure prediction without globally tempering the whole system.
engineering_relevance: The approach reduces the replica-count and traversal bottlenecks of conventional REST, making detailed polymer-assembly modeling more practical for large self-assembling molecular systems.
method: Add one monomer to the growing polymer structure sequentially.
Apply REST only to the newly added monomers to sample binding positions.
Use an energy-based scoring function to select the best configuration at each growth step.
evidence_signal: In an explicit-solvent model supramolecular polymer test, the method reproduced characteristic hydrogen-bonding patterns while improving replica traversal efficiency and lowering computational cost.
watch_item: The method assumes one-dimensional polymerization, so its performance may be limited for systems with branching, competing morphologies, or strong deviations from sequential growth behavior.
paper_title: Sequential replica exchange with solute tempering for atomistic modeling of supramolecular polymer structures
